strategy_summary: The burrows of Australian mice gather dew via a covering of pebbles around the burrow. “The Australian mouse ( Leggadina hermansbergensis ), which lives in a dry and sandy habitat, covers a large area around its burrow with pebbles, apparently for the purpose of increasing the formation of dew as a water supply.”…
mechanism:
The burrows of Australian mice gather dew via a covering of pebbles around the burrow.

“The Australian mouse ( Leggadina hermansbergensis ), which lives in a dry and sandy habitat, covers a large area around its burrow with pebbles, apparently for the purpose of increasing the formation of dew as a water supply.” (Pallasmaa 1995:35)
